libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker image or environment variable appears necessary. Update tests for the 2.0.0 API: use DocumentRenderingContext instead of the removed RenderingContext, avoid enableLogging(), setUsingDefaultTemplate(), and SkinResourceLoader, and create a temporary skin JAR when exercising SiteRenderingContext.setSkin()/copyResources(). For full render/locateDocumentFiles coverage, look up SiteRenderer through a Plexus test container rather than calling new DefaultSiteRenderer().

deterministic_setup.reason: The 2.0.0 renderer exposes skin APIs through org.apache.maven.artifact.Artifact, but the library declares maven-artifact as provided, so it is not pulled transitively for standalone TCK tests.

DefaultSiteRenderer is a Plexus/Sisu component with injected Doxia, parser-module, Velocity, and Plexus fields; upstream 2.0.0 tests use PlexusTest instead of direct construction for meaningful render coverage.
Doxia parser modules are no longer part of doxia-core; the renderer POM notes that modules must be stated so their component descriptors are picked up, and APT is a normal Maven site source format.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8134 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.doxia:doxia-site-renderer:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The reported javac failure is still primarily an API migration in the existing tests; deterministic setup does not replace updating removed class and method usages.
Parser-module dependency is only needed for tests that render or discover APT site documents; simpler value-object or skin-resource tests may not exercise it.
Local Gradle compile/test/native-image verification remains authoritative for whether these dependencies are sufficient.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Doxia Site Renderer 2.0.0 is normally used inside Maven/Plexus with Maven's provided artifact classes and parser-module component descriptors; generated tests that exercise real site/skin rendering need those test-time pieces beyond the basic scaffold.
